questions.question: If you take 10 different random samples from a population with a standard deviation of 5, what can be expected?
options: The mean of the 10 samples will be different each time
The standard deviation of the samples will be 5
The standard deviation of the samples will be less than 5
The standard deviation of the samples will be more than 5
questions.explanation: The standard deviation of the samples will remain the same (5) as the population standard deviation.

questions.question: Which of the following statements is true about taking 10 different random samples?
options: Each sample will have the same mean as the population
Each sample will have a different mean
The mean of the 10 samples will be the same as the population mean
The mean of the 10 samples will be different from the population mean
questions.explanation: The mean of the 10 samples will be the same as the population mean due to the Law of Large Numbers.

questions.question: If you take 10 different random samples from a population that is normally distributed, what can be expected?
options: The samples will not be normally distributed
The samples will be normally distributed
The samples will have a standard deviation greater than 5
The samples will have a standard deviation less than 5
questions.explanation: The samples will be normally distributed since the population is normally distributed.

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit of taking multiple random samples?
options: Increased precision of the sample mean
Increased bias of the sample mean
Increased variability of the sample mean
Decreased bias of the sample mean
questions.explanation: Taking multiple random samples can increase the precision of the sample mean.

questions.question: If you take 10 different random samples from a population with a mean of 10 and a standard deviation of 2, what can be expected?
options: The sample means will be close to 10
The sample means will be far from 10
The sample means will have a standard deviation greater than 2
The sample means will have a standard deviation less than 2
questions.explanation: The sample means will be close to 10 (the population mean) due to the Central Limit Theorem.
